{{Quote|Morals and ethics. Whatever. I survive. Someday I'll start worrying about right and wrong. But for now, I'll worry about the 5,000 credits you're worth.|Taggor Bren|<ref name="GalaxyMag" />}}
'''Taggor Bren''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from the [[planet]] [[Tatooine]] and worked with his family on a [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]] as a [[Youngling|young]] child. One [[Local day/Legends|night]], a group of [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] raided his [[Farm/Legends|farm]] and took Bren. Taggor was raised as one of the Tusken Raider's own, and he was given scars on his face when he was older. After meeting a group of strangers employed by [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], Bren left Tatooine and pursued a career as a bounty hunter.

==Biography==
===Youth on family farm===
Taggor Bren was [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailing]] from<ref name="TCSWE" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>  [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The [[Youngling/Legends|youngest]] member of<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> his<ref name="The Art" /> family, Bren lived and worked on his family's [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]]. On one [[Local day/Legends|night]], Bren's [[Farm/Legends|family farm]] was pillaged by [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]],<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> a culture of [[Desert/Legends|desert]]-dwelling [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] native to Tatooine.<ref name="Encyclopedia-Tusken">{{SWE|groups|tuskenraiders|Tusken Raiders}}</ref> The young boy listened to the Tusken Raiders' wails as they shot through the house's walls. One [[Slug/Legends|bullet]] ricocheted and hit Bren, injuring him. Though having killed his entire family, the invading Tuskens chose not to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Bren, but to instead take him in as one of their own.<ref name="GalaxyMag" />

===Life with the Tusken Raiders and bounty hunting===
[[File:Tusken.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Taggor Bren was raised by the Tusken Raiders (pictured).]]
During his life with the Tusken Raiders, Bren learned their various customs.<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> He<ref name="The Art" /> was taught to speak their [[Language/Legends|language]],<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> [[Tusken]],<ref name="GPBTG">[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']]</ref> and covered his face as the Tuskens did. He formed a bond with his own [[Bantha/Legends|bantha]], [[Hair/Legends|furry]] [[Species/Legends|creatures]]<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> which Tusken Raiders were known to ride.<ref name="Wildlife">''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]''</ref> He believed the bantha would die protecting him, and believed he would do the same contrariwise. Bren went on several raids alongside the Tusken Raiders. When he became older, he was tribally scarred around the top-right of his face, to highlight and represent his right [[Eye/Legends|eye]] as one of Tatooine's twin [[Star/Legends|suns]].<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> 

Bren was given full permission to roam the deserts of Tatooine when he was not needed for raids. On one such roam, Bren came into contact with a [[Unidentified woman (Taggor Bren)|woman]] who was wandering alone, who initially drew back from him. Taggor saw the event as an opportunity to obtain a partner, something which he had long desired. The woman spoke to him in the tongue of his childhood, causing Bren to slowly remember some of the words he had once known. Shortly afterward, three strangers approached and offered Bren [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and supplies in exchange for the woman. He agreed and went with the strangers, curious of the woman's fate.<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> 

Bren later discovered that the strangers were employees of the [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. Taggor eventually left Tatooine in search of power, wealth, and comfort, and he began work as an enforcer<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> in the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]].<ref name="EaWOOG">[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> When he began work as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] sometime after, he ventured out to other [[Star system/Legends|systems]]. After every successful pursuit, Bren cleaned his blade as a way to honor the Tuskens who raised him.<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> 

==Personality and traits==
Bren had [[Color/Legends|blond]] hair, black eyes, fair skin,<ref name="The Art" /> and he stood around 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="Height" /> His black Tusken scars were centered around the upper-right portion of his face.<ref name="The Art" /> As a child, Bren had a sense of right and wrong, but as he grew into his role as a bounty hunter he lost his sense of morals and ethics. While he pursued bounties, Bren preferred to focus on the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] each provided, instead of the morality behind the profession.<ref name="GalaxyMag" />

==Skills and abilities==
He spoke several languages including Tusken and his native language, which he relearned as an enforcer after having long forgotten it.<ref name="GalaxyMag" /><ref name="GPBTG" />

==Equipment==
Taggor Bren wore a traditional tan Tusken [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] with a pouch on the right shoulder and a patch with writing beneath. He also wore a [[Bandolier/Legends|bandolier]], dark-colored pants, a black belt, [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]], and a [[Holster/Legends|holster]]. The [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] carried a large [[Knife/Legends|knife]], a smaller knife located on his bandolier, a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] which he carried on his back, and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] located inside his holster.<ref name="GalaxyMag" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|At the time what I'd planned to do was show a Tusken Raider with his headgear/hood removed so you could see what their faces looked like. The editor loved the idea because they were looking for concepts off the beaten path. I was halfway done with it when my editor called one day and informed me that the concept was a 'no go' -- Lucasfilm decided they wanted the Sand People to remain mysterious, and I really couldn't argue with that logic. I was able to salvage the whole thing by coming up with an alternative concept -- the character would be a bounty hunter indigenous to Tatooine, which explained his look with the respirator, robes, tribal tattoos, etc.|Timothy Bradstreet, on the creation of Taggor Bren|<ref name="SW.com-TB">{{SWArchive|url=eu/explore/profile/f20061129/index.html|text=''Star Wars'' Artist Series: Tim Bradstreet}}</ref>}}
[[File:BF_Salvage_cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Taggor Bren standing next to Boba Fett on the cover of ''Boba Fett'' ½]]
Taggor Bren was created for [[1994 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 2|''Star Wars Galaxy Series 2'']], a trading card set released by [[Topps|The Topps Company, Inc.]]<ref name="TimBrad-Card">{{Topps|set=1994 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 2|cardname=Tim Bradstreet}}</ref> in [[1994]].<ref name="TOTTCS">''[[Star Wars Galaxy: The Original Topps Trading Card Series]]''</ref> Artist [[Timothy Bradstreet]] was asked to illustrate a card for the set about himself, titled "Tim Bradstreet." Bradstreet's original concept for Bren was a Tusken Raider without headgear, to reveal what the Tusken Raiders' faces looked like. The set's editor fancied the concept as Topps was looking for something unusual, however [[Lucasfilm]] shot down the idea, as they wanted the Tusken Raiders to remain mysterious. To salvage his idea, Bradstreet reworked his character to instead establish Bren as a human raised by Tuskens.<ref name="SW.com-TB" /> 

Taggor Bren received his name and official backstory in "[[Taggor Bren: One Dangerous Bounty Hunter]]," a starter story for the [[West End Games]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]], written by [[Bill Smith]] and published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]'' in [[October]] 1994.<ref name="GalaxyMag" /> Despite not appearing in the comic itself, Taggor Bren appeared on the cover of [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Boba Fett ½|''Boba Fett'' ½]], standing next to notorious bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] on a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] lineup. The cover was drawn by Bradstreet,<ref name="BF0.5">[[Boba Fett ½|''Boba Fett'' ½]]</ref> and the comic was released on [[December 1]], [[1997]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54023/star_wars_boba_fett_1997_0.5|text=Star Wars: Boba Fett (1997) #0.5}}</ref>
